concept.title: Multi-Channel Output Formats
concept.shortdesc: DITA-OT transforms structured source into HTML5, PDF, EPUB, Markdown, and custom formats — each from the same map with no content duplication.

section.title: One source, many outputs
section.p: The core value of DITA publishing is single-source, multi-channel delivery. You author once in structured XML, then transform the same content into every format your audience needs — without maintaining parallel copies.
DITA-OT ships with built-in transforms (called transtypes) and supports additional formats through installable plugins.

section.title: Built-in transtypes
section.p: DITA-OT includes these transforms out of the box:

row.entry: Modern HTML5 with responsive navigation, search index, and CSS hooks
XSL-FO pipeline rendered via Apache FOP — produces print-ready PDFs
Legacy XHTML 1.0 output for older systems
Exports DITA content back to Markdown for developer workflows
HTML with JavaScript-driven table of contents

section.title: Plugin-based formats
section.p: The DITA-OT plugin registry and community provide additional transtypes:

ul.li: EPUB — org.dita.epub generates EPUB 3 eBooks for e-readers and mobile reading apps.
Normalized DITA — org.dita.normalize resolves all conrefs and keyrefs into flat, standalone topics for CMS import.
JSON / YAML — Community plugins can export topic metadata and content as structured data for API documentation or chatbot ingestion.
Custom HTML5 — Branded plugins (like com.extense.html5.branded) extend the built-in HTML5 transform with custom CSS, headers, footers, and scripts.
Custom PDF — Branded plugins (like com.extense.pdf.branded) override FO templates for corporate fonts, logos, and page layouts.

section.title: Choosing the right format
section.p: Match the output format to the delivery channel:

ul.li: Web portal → HTML5 (with branded plugin for navigation and styling)
Printed manual or regulatory submission → PDF
E-reader / offline mobile → EPUB
Developer repository → Markdown (GitHub-flavored)
AI / chatbot pipeline → JSON export or normalized DITA for RAG ingestion
CMS migration → Normalized DITA with all references resolved

section.p: You can — and should — build multiple formats from the same map in a single CI pipeline. This is the write-once-publish-everywhere promise of DITA.

ul.li: Use args.cssroot and args.css to inject custom stylesheets.
The args.hdr and args.ftr parameters add header/footer includes.
Search indexes are generated automatically — enable with args.indexshow=yes.
PDF generation uses Apache FOP by default. For higher fidelity, consider Antenna House or RenderX XEP.
Use args.chapter.layout=MINITOC to add mini table-of-contents at chapter starts.
Custom fonts require FOP configuration changes (see the XSLT customization part of this guide).
Cover images: set via args.epub.coverimage.
EPUB 3 supports embedded video and audio — DITA <object> elements map automatically.
Markdown export is lossy — DITA metadata, conrefs, and keys do not survive the round-trip.
Use markdown export for developer consumption, not as an authoring format.

section.title: Building multiple formats from one map
section.p: Use a DITA-OT project file (.xml) to define multiple deliverables in a single build:
section.codeblock: <project xmlns="https://www.dita-ot.org/project"> <context name="training-guide"> <input href="extense-supermap.ditamap"/> </context> <deliverable name="html5"> <context idref="training-guide"/> <output href="./output/html5"/> <publication transtype="html5"> <param name="args.css" value="brand.css"/> </publication> </deliverable> <deliverable name="pdf"> <context idref="training-guide"/> <output href="./output/pdf"/> <publication transtype="pdf"> <param name="args.chapter.layout" value="MINITOC"/> </publication> </deliverable> </project>
